projectDescription: A real-time, multi-domain OSINT dashboard that aggregates live data from dozens of open-source intelligence feeds — aircraft, ships, satellites, conflict zones, earthquakes, and more — rendered on a unified dark-ops map interface.
keyFeatures: Real-time aviation tracking — commercial, private, military aircraft via ADS-B
Maritime AIS vessel stream with 25,000+ ships via WebSocket
Orbital satellite tracking with SGP4 propagation and mission-type classification
GDELT-powered geopolitical conflict events and Ukraine frontline
GPS jamming zone detection via NAC-P degradation analysis
2,000+ live CCTV camera feeds from global traffic networks
NASA FIRMS fire hotspots and NOAA space weather monitoring
Right-click region dossier with Wikidata/Wikipedia intelligence
Sentinel-2 satellite imagery on-demand via Microsoft Planetary Computer
KiwiSDR public radio receiver network with embedded tuner
Internet outage monitoring via Georgia Tech IODA
Docker/Podman single-command deployment and Helm/Kubernetes support
publicApiSurface: GET /api/live-data/fast — fast-update layer data (flights, ships, satellites) with bbox filtering
GET /api/live-data/slow — slow-update layer data (news, earthquakes, fires) with bbox filtering
GET /api/health — service health and data source counts
GET /api/refresh — force a full data refresh
GET /api/region-dossier?lat=&lng= — country/city intelligence for a coordinate
GET /api/sentinel2/search?lat=&lng= — latest Sentinel-2 imagery for a point
GET /api/radio/nearest?lat=&lng= — nearest OpenMHz trunked radio system
POST /api/ais/feed — ingest AIS-catcher decoded vessel messages
POST /api/viewport — update map viewport bbox for AIS stream filtering
